subject: DevOps
title: Declarative: Describe the Goal
description: A quick, practical guide to Declarative: Describe the Goal.

hook.text: You say: 'I want a VPC with two subnets and a load balancer.' The tool figures out how to create them.

buildup.text: Declarative IaC describes the desired end state. The tool compares it to reality and makes the minimum changes.

discovery.text: If a subnet already exists, the tool skips it. If it's missing, the tool creates it. Idempotent by design.

twist.text: Run the same code twice and nothing changes the second time. The desired state already matches reality.

climax.text: Terraform, CloudFormation, and Pulumi are declarative. They plan changes, show a diff, and apply only what's needed.

punchline.text: Declare what you want. The tool computes the delta and applies it.

quiz.question: What does idempotent mean in the context of IaC?
options: Running the same code multiple times produces the same result
The code runs faster each time it's executed
Changes are applied in random order
